mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9% signals a deeply distressed labor market, suppressing consumer demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative forward returns as investors anticipate prolonged economic weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 10.0% as of latest reading, near post-war highs
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE is above 9.0% during the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) environment, rising inflation signals reflation and successful monetary stimulus rather than a tightening risk. An increase in CPIAUCSL suggests growing aggregate demand, which is bullish for corporate revenues and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has maintained ZIRP since late 2008
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in CPIAUCSL is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in retail sales reflects strengthening consumer balance sheets and confidence. A positive 3-month rate of change in RSAFS directly implies higher expected corporate revenues, driving positive equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales have been recovering since the trough in early 2009
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) for 20-day returns remains below 0.02 for any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates that the manufacturing and supply side of the economy is recovering, leading to higher capacity utilization and corporate profitability. This positive momentum is a leading indicator for earnings and drives positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been rising steadily since its June 2009 low
failure_condition: Periods of positive 6-month change in INDPRO are not followed by statistically significant positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A durable economic recovery requires both job creation and consumer spending. A positive signal is generated only when both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit positive 3-month momentum, indicating a self-sustaining recovery that boosts equity markets.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in PAYEMS > 0 AND 3-month change in RSAFS > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Job losses have slowed but are not yet consistently positive
failure_condition: The strategy of buying the S&P 500 only when both signals are positive fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key long-leading indicator of economic health. A sharp, sustained rebound in HOUST from deeply depressed levels signals a bottoming in the housing crisis and renewed construction activity, which has broad positive spillovers for the economy and equity market sentiment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST collapsed during the GFC and has been volatile near historic lows
failure_condition: Positive trigger events are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of cases during the walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely accommodative monetary policy, proxied by a near-zero federal funds rate, lowers the discount rate for future corporate earnings and encourages risk-taking. This provides a persistent tailwind for equity valuations, leading to positive returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently 0.12%, within the 0-0.25% target range
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% is not statistically different from the average return when it is above 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, the rate of improvement in the labor market is more important than the absolute level. A larger-than-expected monthly drop in the unemployment rate signals that the recovery is gaining traction, triggering a positive revision of growth expectations and immediate positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE drops by more than 0.1 percentage points in a month are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns on average.
